lesson_title: Using function templates in multiple files

cards.front: Where should function template definitions be placed?
cards.back: In header files, not in .cpp files. The full template definition must be visible wherever it is instantiated.
cards.source: 11.10 - Using function templates in multiple files

cards.text: Unlike regular functions, templates are not compiled until they are {{c1::instantiated}}.
cards.source: 11.10 - Using function templates in multiple files

cards.front: Why can't you put function template definitions in .cpp files like regular functions?
cards.back: Because the compiler needs to see the complete template definition at the point of instantiation to generate code for each specific type used.
cards.source: 11.10 - Using function templates in multiple files

cards.concept: How to organize function templates across multiple files
cards.explanation: Place the entire template definition (including the body) in a header file. Include this header wherever you need to use the template. This is different from regular functions where you typically separate declaration (in .h) from definition (in .cpp).
cards.source: 11.10 - Using function templates in multiple files

cards.text: Best practice: Template definitions should go in {{c1::header files}} so they are accessible wherever they are needed.
cards.source: 11.10 - Using function templates in multiple files

cards.front: Will the same template instantiated with the same type in multiple .cpp files cause linker errors?
cards.back: No. Template instantiations with the same type are considered identical and the linker will keep only one copy.
cards.source: 11.10 - Using function templates in multiple files

cards.question: What's wrong with this organization?
cards.answer: The template definition is in the .cpp file, but it needs to be in the header file where it can be seen by all files that use it.
cards.explanation: This organization works for regular functions but not templates, because templates need their full definition visible at instantiation time.
cards.source: 11.10 - Using function templates in multiple files

cards.front: What happens if a template definition is not available when the compiler tries to instantiate it?
cards.back: The compiler will issue an error because it cannot generate the code without seeing the complete template definition.
cards.source: 11.10 - Using function templates in multiple files

cards.text: The compiler instantiates templates in {{c1::each translation unit}} where they are used, but the linker removes duplicates.
cards.source: 11.10 - Using function templates in multiple files

cards.front: Is it acceptable to define function templates entirely in header files?
cards.back: Yes, this is the standard and recommended practice for function templates. Unlike regular functions, this does not violate the one definition rule.
cards.source: 11.10 - Using function templates in multiple files
